title: Mentoring Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Youth Services > Mentoring Programs 59 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Disabilities Issues Homeless Youth LGBTQ2+ Issues ADAPT INTEGRATED HEALTH CARE ROSEBURG PREVENTION & EDUCATION PREVENTION AND EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community prevention education for substance misuse, gambling/gaming, and suicide. Also offers mentoring programs for youth with a focus on building confidence and life skills. 546 SE Jackson Street Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Education: Unrestricted Youth Mentoring: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 672-2691 2701 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ADAPT INTEGRATED HEALTH CARE YOUTH OUTPATIENT PREVENTION AND EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community prevention education for substance misuse, gambling/gaming, and suicide. Also offers mentoring programs for youth with a focus on building confidence and life skills. 548 SE Jackson Street Roseburg, OR 974705412 Eligibility: Education: Unrestricted Youth Mentoring: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 672-2691 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ANTFARM AXIS LEARNING CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a youth education center with academic and youth support services. Provides 13 computers to create, facilitate learning, and access to information. Assisting young people with completion of high school, establishment of educational goals, and movement towards college and/or work readiness are core services offered. Community members offer support through tutoring, mentoring, community volunteering, job-skills training, supported employment, and assisting young people with problem solving and critical thinking skills. 39140 Proctor Boulevard Sandy, OR 97055 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 668-9955 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ARK PROJECT OF COOS COUNTY ARK OF COOS COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides basic needs, essential support services, crisis intervention, and advocacy. Services include a drop-in center, food pantry, children's clothing, school supplies, personal grooming supplies, feminine hygiene supplies, mentoring, information and referrals for other assistance agencies, and assistance in applying for identification. 817 S 10th Street Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Ages 0-21 and their families who are experiencing homelessness Hours: School year: Monday-Thursday 1pm-5pm Summer: Tuesday-Wednesday 1pm-5pm (541) 267-3104 7115 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ARTISTS MENTORSHIP PROGRAM AMP DROP-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to music lessons, art workshops, recording studio sessions, roleplaying game sessions, cultural outings, meals, hygiene supplies, clothing, first aid supplies, and mentorship services. 16 NW Broadway Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Homeless youth ages 15-25 Hours: Monday-Thursday 4pm-8pm, Friday by appointment only Email (503) 481-4970 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BAMBINOS OREGON MENTORING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Mentoring program with Christian women. 322 Main Street Suite 200 Dallas, OR 97338 Eligibility: Women Hours: Monday/Thursday 9am-noon, Tuesday 12pm-6pm, 1st and 3rd Saturday of the month 1pm-4pm Email (503) 623-4618 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BASIC RIGHTS OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Builds and supports political policies that promote equality for lesbian, gay, bisexual, and transgender Oregonians. Provides various services including information and referral to legal services, youth mentorship programs, education and outreach to the business community, political advocacy, and offers volunteer opportunities. Eligibility: Individuals with interest in LGBTQ2+ issues Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 222-6151 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BIG BROTHERS BIG SISTERS COLUMBIA NORTHWEST BIG BROTHERS BIG SISTERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF One-to-one mentor program matches youth with an adult mentor to serve as a positive role model and offer guidance and support. 6443 SW Beaverton Hillsdale Highway Suite 200 Portland, OR 97221 Eligibility: Participants: Ages 6-18 Mentors: Age 18 and older, must pass a background check, and commit to 2-4 outings per month for at least one year Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-7pm Email (503) 249-4859 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BIG BROTHERS BIG SISTERS OF CENTRAL OREGON BIG BROTHERS BIG SISTERS MENTORING PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides one-on-one mentoring to children through screened and trained volunteers. Mentors spend time with children reading books, playing sports, helping with homework, various outings, giving advice and inspiration. 62895 Hamby Road Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Ages 6-18 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 312-6047 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BLACK AFFINITY GROUP BLACK AFFINITY GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Facilitates affinity groups in schools, workplaces, and community spaces. These groups provide a safe space for participants to talk about navigating work, school, and daily life in white-dominant environments. Eligibility: Individuals who identify as Black or Mixed with most programs for ages 10-17 who identify as Black and male (cisgender, transgender, and non-binary) Hours: Varies by event Email (971) 319-1194 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOYS & GIRLS CLUB OF EMERALD VALLEY BOYS & GIRLS CLUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a positive space for youth to engage in fun and empowering programs, such as leadership and community service, after-school programs, academic enrichment, workforce training, performing and visual arts, camps, and personal health and fitness. 1545 W 22nd Avenue Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Ages 6-18 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5:30pm Email (541) 345-9939 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BRIDGES TO CHANGE CLACKAMAS COUNTY OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers transitional housing, treatment, and supportive services for individuals recovering from addiction. Programs are led by peer mentors. Intensive outpatient (IOP) and outpatient substance use disorder treatment are available at multiple locations. 707 Main Street Suite 220 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (503) 560-0624 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BRIDGES TO CHANGE SPECIAL PROGRAMS OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers transitional housing, treatment, and supportive services for individuals recovering from addiction. Programs are led by peer mentors. Intensive outpatient (IOP) and outpatient substance use disorder treatment are available at multiple locations. 229 NE 181st Avenue Portland, OR 97230 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 465-2749 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BRIDGES TO CHANGE WASHINGTON COUNTY OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers transitional housing, treatment, and supportive services for individuals recovering from addiction. Programs are led by peer mentors. Intensive outpatient (IOP) and outpatient substance use disorder treatment are available at multiple locations. 1049 SW Baseline Street Suite D480 Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (971) 386-3443 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CANBY CENTER EDUCATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides after school tutoring, reading mentors, and finance, cooking, and sewing life skills classes. Eligibility: Grades K-12 for after school tutoring, unrestricted for all other programs Hours: Varies by class Email (503) 266-2920 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK BEND OFFICE DISABILITY RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational parenting support, family support group sessions, education and mentoring support, and referral to other resources as needed. This empowers individuals with disabilities, and their families, to take charge of their life's direction. Also assists in determining supports, goals, and community inclusion, resulting in increased independence. 2525 NE Twin Knolls Drive Suite 7 Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Families and children with disabilities Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 548-8559 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK KLAMATH FALLS OFFICE DISABILITY RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational parenting support, family support group sessions, education and mentoring support, and referral to other resources as needed. This empowers individuals with disabilities, and their families, to take charge of their life's direction. Also assists in determining supports, goals, and community inclusion, resulting in increased independence. 2701 Foothills Boulevard Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Families and children with disabilities Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 548-8559 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK WARM SPRINGS OFFICE DISABILITY RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational parenting support, family support group sessions, education and mentoring support, and referral to other resources as needed. This empowers individuals with disabilities, and their families, to take charge of their life's direction. Also assists in determining supports, goals, and community inclusion, resulting in increased independence. 1257 Kot-Num Road Warm Springs, OR 97761 Eligibility: Families and children with disabilities Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 548-8559 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHILDREN'S CANCER ASSOCIATION JOYRX MENTORSHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Pairs seriously ill children and teens undergoing treatment with trained adult volunteer mentors. Mentors provide companionship through shared activities such as games, music, and hobbies. This support also gives parents valuable time to meet with doctors, connect with family and friends, or simply take a needed break. 1200 NW Naito Parkway Suite 140 Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Families with children in treatment for cancer or other serious illnesses ages 0-18 at Randall Children's Hospital at Legacy Emanuel or Doernbecher Children's Hospital Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-1:30pm. Mentors meet with families based on both parties' schedules. Email (503) 200-5113 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONNECTED LANE COUNTY SPARK AT BOOTH KELLY DROP-IN SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a youth drop-in center. Services include job finding assistance, resume and cover letter support, interview practice, career mentorship, assistance with completing college and scholarship applications, information and referral to housing and healthcare community resources, and access to shower and laundry facilities. 303 S 5th Street Suite 150 Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Ages 14-24 Hours: Wednesday-Thursday 3pm-6pm, Friday varies by month Email (541) 654-4095 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DESCHUTES COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE CENTRAL OREGON PARTNERSHIPS FOR YOUTH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a one-on-one volunteer mentorship program aimed at reducing trauma for children impacted by parental incarceration. Mentors commit to spending a few hours each week for at least one year with a child. Activities may include attending community events, participating in sports or recreational activities (such as biking, playing at the park, art projects, swimming, bowling, hiking), or visiting the library or movies. Some mentors provide academic support, help build reading skills, and connect children and families to additional community resources, including scholarship opportunities and youth enrichment programs. 63333 W Highway 20 Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Ages 5-15 with an incarcerated parent or caregiver Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 388-6655 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAITH HOPE AND CHARITY HEALTH OPPORTUNITY PROSPERITY AND EMPOWERMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer support mentoring, certified recovery mentoring, assistance with applications for birth certificates, Social Security, and social services. Can provide basic needs navigation. 460 SW Madison Avenue Suite 1 Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 740-5807 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAITH HOPE AND CHARITY FAITH HOPE AND CHARITY ALBANY HEALTH OPPORTUNITY PROSPERITY AND EMPOWERMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer support mentoring, certified recovery mentoring, assistance with applications for birth certificates, Social Security, and social services. Can provide basic needs navigation. 635 SW 3rd Street Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 619-4584 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FOR THE CHILDREN ROYAL FAMILY KIDS CAMP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides camps and mentoring programs for children who have experienced abuse, neglect, or abandonment. Offers individual attention with one counselor for every one or two children. Locator Santa Ana, CA 92704 Eligibility: Children ages 6-12 in foster care with an active child welfare case Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (714) 438-2494 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GIRLS INC OF THE PACIFIC NORTHWEST AFTER SCHOOL EMPOWERMENT PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides school-based empowerment programs and assertiveness education, including: -Allies in Action: Uses an interactive, peer-centered approach to reduce competition and hostility among girls -Friendly PEERsuasion: Addresses drug abuse prevention as a peer issue, encouraging young people to model healthy behaviors -Operation SMART: Mobilizes community resources to support girls -Economic Literacy: Guides girls in making sound financial decisions and building economic stability. These programs help girls feel more confident and capable in school by fostering self-esteem, collaborative learning, and other key developmental assets. 4800 S Macadam Avenue Suite 309 Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Girls ages 6-18. Girls must attend the program associated with their school; participating schools vary. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 230-0054 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GUIDANCE 2 GREATNESS BEYOND REENTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists with criminal record and eviction expungement, job development, workplace training, financial literacy, felony rights, and reentry training. May be able to provide program participants with supplies such as personal protective equipment (PPE) and a desktop computer. Eligibility: -Age 18 and older -Reentry Training for women ages 16-24 -Focus on the African American and BIPOC community Hours: Not applicable Email (503) 575-7612 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEARTS WITH A MISSION HEARTS WITH A MISSION MEDFORD YOUTH SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Youth shelter providing temporary emergency shelter for up to 120 days with written parent/guardian consent or 72 hours without written consent. Services for sheltered and unsheltered youth include case management, mentoring, housing search assistance, and counseling services. Bathing facilities, laundry facilities, a clothing closet, and personal grooming supplies are also provided. School supplies are for enrolled participants only. 517 Edwards Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Homeless or at-risk youth ages 10-17 Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 646-7385 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HIVES FOR HEROES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a sustainability and stewardship focused mentorship and volunteer opportunity. "NewBees" (new mentees) receive one-on-one training in beekeeping and conservation while connecting with peers, community, and nature and working towards operating their own hives and provided mentorship to future "NewBees." Eligibility: Mentees: Military personnel, veterans, and first responders (active or retired), including firefighters, police officers, and paramedics Mentors: 3 years of beekeeping experience Hours: Varies by location Email (208) 863-9875 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOSEA YOUTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides meals, clothing, laundry, personal hygiene items, showers, job training classes, mentoring programs, referrals to other resources, and access to telephone and computer services. For the winter season, offering tents, sleeping bags, and blankets. 834 Monroe Street Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 with a focus on individuals experiencing or at risk of homelessness Hours: Monday-Thursday 11am-6:30pm, Friday 11am-3pm Lunch: noon-2pm Dinner: 5pm Email (541) 344-5583 - Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION YOUTH MENTORING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides one-on-one mentoring for students. 10301 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Grades K-12 Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (971) 271-6552 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION IRCO AFRICA HOUSE YOUTH MENTORING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides one-on-one mentoring for students. 709 NE 102nd Avenue Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Grades K-12 Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm (971) 271-6552 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION IRCO PACIFIC ISLANDER AND ASIAN FAMILY CENTER YOUTH MENTORING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides one-on-one mentoring for students. 8040 NE Sandy Boulevard Suite 200 Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Grades K-12 Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 560-9721 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JACKSON STREET YOUTH SERVICES MENTORING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides youth-centered mentoring with a volunteer matched to provide a connection and guidance. Matched participants and mentors meet one-on-one weekly, or for approximately four hours per month, with a one-year commitment. 225 SW 4th Street Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Ages 10-17 with a legal guardian giving consent to participate Hours: Varies Email (541) 360-0861 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LAKE COUNTY EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT YOUTH MENTOR PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Links mentors and youth one-on-one to encourage academic and personal success in a casual atmosphere. Youth work with adults for one hour per week to develop life skills, such as setting boundaries, time management, improved study habits, strong communication, positive peer relationships, and respect for self and others. 357 N L Street Lakeview, OR 97630 Eligibility: Mentors must pass a background and fingerprint check Mentees must be students in grades K-12 Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-3pm (541) 947-4880 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LATINO NETWORK MILPA GUN VIOLENCE PREVENTION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers case management and mentorship for youth at risk of community violence. Family resources available to promote positive choices and prevent violence. 410 NE 18th Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Ages 13-21 Latine and their families Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 830-1554 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LATINO NETWORK PADRINOS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mentorship services for youth facing challenges in their schools and communities who are at low to mid-risk of community violence. Mentors offer support with goal setting, school success, career planning, and personal development. Can also provide a connection to community resources and activities. 410 NE 18th Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Age 13-18 Latine with a family member impacted by the justice system Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 830-1554 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC NEWBERG TRANSFORMATIONAL MINISTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a Christian faith-based mentoring program that connects people with church members to help them set goals, develop skills, manage finances, and access information and community resources. Participants are mentored through classes and one-on-one sessions for 8 to 12 weeks. 200 S College Street Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Serves current Love INC clients Hours: Tuesday 5:45pm-8pm Email (503) 537-3999 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOWER COLUMBIA Q CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides resources and support services to the LGBTQ2SIA+ community and allies of the Lower Columbia region. Services include a community center, education opportunities, mentorship, and monthly support groups for adults, youth, and families. Limited financial assistance is available as funds allow for gender-affirming costs, name changes, hormone treatments, and other expenses approved on a case-by-case basis. Financial assistance is limited to once per year, with a $200 limit. 171 W Bond Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Community Center walk in hours: Tuesday 2pm-5pm, Thursday noon-4pm. Other hours vary by service. Email (503) 468-5155 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MANO A MANO FAMILY CENTER COLONIA AMISTAD POSITIVE YOUTH DEVELOPMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides youth with mentoring, tutoring, and case management to help them graduate, find stable employment, and housing. Offers after-school programs, volunteer opportunities, and leadership development. 30 S Gun Club Road Independence, OR 97351 Eligibility: Unrestricted Strengthening Families: Ages 10-14 and their parents Hours: Monday-Thursday noon-6pm Email (503) 315-2290 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MANO A MANO FAMILY CENTER COLONIA LIBERTAD POSITIVE YOUTH DEVELOPMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides youth with mentoring, tutoring, and case management to help them graduate, find stable employment, and housing. Offers after-school programs, volunteer opportunities, and leadership development. 2921 Saddle Club Street SE #1009 Salem, OR 97317 Eligibility: Unrestricted Strengthening Families: Ages 10-14 and their parents Hours: Monday-Thursday noon-6pm Email (503) 315-2290 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MANO A MANO FAMILY CENTER NORTHGATE CENTER POSITIVE YOUTH DEVELOPMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides youth with mentoring, tutoring, and case management to help them graduate, find stable employment, and housing. Offers after-school programs, volunteer opportunities, and leadership development. 3850 Portland Road NE Suite 130 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Strengthening Families: Ages 10-14 and their parents Hours: Monday-Thursday noon-6pm Email (503) 363-1895 Get Directions Visit Website More Details METROPOLITAN FAMILY SERVICE SUN YOUTH ADVOCACY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides school-linked, age-appropriate, and culturally specific academic support activities such as tutoring, mentoring, reading clubs, gender groups, conflict resolution classes, case management, family engagement, and skill-building groups. 1700 NW Civic Drive Suite 200 Gresham, OR 97080 Eligibility: Grades 5-12 who are at risk of academic failure and their families Hours: Varies Email (971) 506-2609 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH NEW DAY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Supports the safety, needs, and rights of people experiencing sex trafficking or exploitation, trading sex, or who are at risk. Provides case outreach, case management/advocacy, and counseling. Helps with safety planning, transportation, and access to shelter or other housing resources. Also offers referral to additional resources, access to employment and education programs, and LGBTQ2+ resources. 314 SW 9th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 12-25 who have been impacted by sex trafficking Hours: Referral Line: Monday-Friday 10am-6pm Hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 258-7081 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH YOUTH OPPORTUNITY CENTER ALBA COLLABORATIVE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides short-term emergency shelter, mentorship, family mediation, resource referrals, and support with basic needs, including food, clothing, showers, and hygiene supplies with a focus on LGBTQIA2S+ youth. 470 SE 165th Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Ages 9-17 and experiencing housing instability Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (971) 754-4350 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW HARVEST CHURCH HARVEST HUSKIES AFTER-SCHOOL PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a faith-based after-school program that includes mentoring, homework assistance, and enrichment activities. Space is limited. 4290 Portland Road NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Students who are enrolled at Hallman Elementary School Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 2pm-4:30pm Email (503) 763-6911 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEXT DOOR GORGE YOUTH MENTORING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Recruits, screens, trains, and supports adult volunteer mentors for children and teens. Mentors offer friendship and support with the goal of helping youth perform better in school and making them less likely to use drugs or turn to violence to solve problems. BBBS matches mentors with youth in their own community. 965 Tucker Road Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Ages 7-14 can be matched with an adult mentor Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 490-9979 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEXT DOOR YOUTH FAMILY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support services, including counseling, support groups, violence and anger prevention training, life skills groups, parenting classes, new parenting groups, crime victims support, community information and referral and temporary housing. 965 Tucker Road Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Ages 6-21 and their families Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 386-6665 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEXT DOOR NEXT DOOR WASCO GORGE YOUTH MENTORING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Recruits, screens, trains, and supports volunteer adult mentors for children and teens. Mentors offer friendship and support with the goal of helping youth perform better in school and making them less likely to use drugs or turn to violence to solve problems. Matches mentors with youth in their own community. 1113 Kelly Avenue The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Ages 7-14 can be matched with an adult mentor. Directions Visit Website More Details P:EAR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center that offers arts and music, mentoring, educational, and outdoor recreational programs. Additional supportive services include bus tickets, haircuts, clothing, and support for pregnancy and parenting. Also offers meals and cooking classes. Accepts donations of food, clothing, and camping gear. 338 NW 6th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 15-24 experiencing homelessness Hours: Tuesday-Friday: 9am-2pm Breakfast at 9am and lunch at noon to those served by p:ear Donations: Monday-Friday 8am-3pm Email (503) 228-6677 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SELF ENHANCEMENT INC YOUTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers in-school case management and support as well as after-school enrochment programs. Services include mentoring, case management, parenting classes, and referrals to outside resources. Parent coordinators work with family service staff to help parents find housing, employment, and counseling in order to provide a safe environment for their children. 3920 N Kerby Avenue Portland, OR 97227 Eligibility: Grades 2nd-12th and attendence at partnering school. Check website for list of partnering schools. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-7pm, Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 249-1721 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEQUOIA MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES BEHAVIORAL HEALTH RESOURCE NETWORK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides low barrier treatment services for drug and alcohol addiction, assessment, treatment planning, individual/group counseling, case management, peer support, mentoring, specimen testing, supported employment, and housing resources. 4585 SW 185th Avenue Aloha, OR 97078 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday - Thursday 9am to 7pm and Friday 9am to 5pm Email (503) 591-9280 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEQUOIA MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES SEQUOIA MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES HILLSBORO BEHAVIORAL HEALTH RESOURCE NETWORK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides low barrier treatment services for drug and alcohol addiction, assessment, treatment planning, individual/group counseling, case management, peer support, mentoring, specimen testing, supported employment, and housing resources. 395 W Main Street Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday - Thursday 9am to 7pm and Friday 9am to 5pm Email (503) 591-9280 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SRIPONYA COLLECTIVE PEER SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides young people with mental health challenges with support from trained young adults who have similar lived experiences. 29 SE D Street Madras, OR 97741 Eligibility: Age 18 and younger, must have OHP Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 10am-6pm during the school year. Email (541) 498-0968 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE HEALING CIRCLE INC MENTORING PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a year-round mentoring program, activities may include roller skating, craft activities, scavenger hunts, general hangouts, and youth-directed activities. 1054 Exchange Street Suite A Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Children who are survivors of sexual abuse, a court case is not required. Most activities are for children ages 5-20. Children outside the age range can be accommodated on a case-by-case basis. Hours: Varies Email (503) 468-2844 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YOUNG LIFE YOUNGLIVES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Christian-based mentoring program where mentors accompany teens to parenting classes, offer club meetings and groups on Bible study, group outings and life skill instruction. Also offers a six-day camp with child care is included.
